def solve(m, n, waitCost)
  # Initialize the priority queue
  pq = []
  # Initial state: (0,0) at time 1, cost is 1
  # The cost is entry cost of (0,0)
  initial_cost = (0+1) * (0+1)
  # Push (cost, i, j, time)
  pq << [initial_cost, 0, 0, 1]
  # Track the minimal cost for each (i,j, time)
  # Since Ruby doesn't have a 3D array, we can use a hash
  visited = Hash.new
  # The visited hash will store the minimal cost for (i,j, time)
  # So, for each (i,j, time), the value is the minimal cost to reach it
  # We can use a key like [i, j, time]
  # Initialize the visited with the initial state
  visited[[0,0,1]] = initial_cost
  # Directions for movement: right and down
  directions = [[0,1], [1,0]]
  # While the queue is not empty
  while pq.length > 0
    # Sort the queue to get the minimal cost
    pq.sort!
    # Pop the first element
    current = pq.shift
    cost, i, j, t = current
    # Check if we reached the destination
    if i == m-1 && j == n-1
      return cost
    end
    # Check if this state has been processed with a lower cost
    # If the current cost is higher than the stored one, skip
    key = [i, j, t]
    if visited[key] && visited[key] < cost
      next
    end
    # Process based on the parity of t
    if t % 2 == 1 # odd time: move
      # Check possible moves
      directions.each do |di, dj|
        ni = i + di
        nj = j + dj
        # Check if the new cell is within bounds
        if ni >= 0 && ni < m && nj >= 0 && nj < n
          new_time = t + 1
          new_cost = cost + (ni+1) * (nj+1)
          # Check if this state is already in visited with a lower cost
          key_new = [ni, nj, new_time]
          if visited[key_new].nil? || visited[key_new] > new_cost
            visited[key_new] = new_cost
            pq << [new_cost, ni, nj, new_time]
          end
        end
      end
    else # even time: wait
      # Wait in the current cell
      new_time = t + 1
      new_cost = cost + waitCost[i][j]
      key_new = [i, j, new_time]
      if visited[key_new].nil? || visited[key_new] > new_cost
        visited[key_new] = new_cost
        pq << [new_cost, i, j, new_time]
      end
    end
  end
  # If the queue is empty and destination not found (should not happen)
  return -1
end

def solve(m, n, waitCost)
  # Initialize the priority queue
  pq = []
  # Initial state: (0,0) at time 1, cost is 1
  # The cost is entry cost of (0,0)
  initial_cost = (0+1) * (0+1)
  # Push (cost, i, j, time)

  # Track the minimal cost for each (i,j, time)
  # Since Ruby doesn't have a 3D array, we can use a hash
  visited = Hash.new
  # The visited hash will store the minimal cost for (i,j, time)
  # So, for each (i,j, time), the value is the minimal cost to reach it
  # We can use a key like [i, j, time]
  # Initialize the visited with the initial state
  visited[[0,0,1]] = initial_cost
  # Directions for movement: right and down
  directions = [[0,1], [1,0]]
  # While the queue is not empty
  while pq.length > 0
    # Sort the queue to get the minimal cost
    pq.sort!
    # Pop the first element
    current = pq.shift
    cost, i, j, t = current
    # Check if we reached the destination
    if i == m-1 && j == n-1
      return cost
    end
    # Check if this state has been processed with a lower cost
    # If the current cost is higher than the stored one, skip
    key = [i, j, t]
    if visited[key] && visited[key] < cost
      next
    end
    # Process based on the parity of t
    if t % 2 == 1 # odd time: move
      # Check possible moves
      directions.each do |di, dj|
        ni = i + di
        nj = j + dj
        # Check if the new cell is within bounds
        if ni >= 0 && ni < m && nj >= 0 && nj < n
          new_time = t + 1
          new_cost = cost + (ni+1) * (nj+1)
          # Check if this state is already in visited with a lower cost
          key_new = [ni, nj, new_time]
          if visited[key_new].nil? || visited[key_new] > new_cost
            visited[key_new] = new_cost
            pq << [new_cost, ni, nj, new_time]
          end
        end
      end
    else # even time: wait
      # Wait in the current cell
      new_time = t + 1
      new_cost = cost + waitCost[i][j]
      key_new = [i, j, new_time]
      if visited[key_new].nil? || visited[key_new] > new_cost
        visited[key_new] = new_cost
        pq << [new_cost, i, j, new_time]
      end
    end
  end
  # If the queue is empty and destination not found (should not happen)
  return -1

def solve(m, n, waitCost)
  pq = []
  # Initial state: (0,0) at time 1, cost is (0+1)*(0+1) = 1
  initial_cost = (0+1) * (0+1)
  pq << [initial_cost, 0, 0, 1]
  visited = {}
  visited[[0, 0, 1]] = initial_cost
  directions = [[0, 1], [1, 0]]
  while pq.length > 0
    # Sort the queue to get the minimal cost
    pq.sort!
    current = pq.shift
    cost, i, j, t = current
    # Check if we reached the destination
    if i == m-1 && j == n-1
      return cost
    end
    # Check if this state has been processed with a lower cost
    key = [i, j, t]
    if visited[key] && visited[key] < cost
      next
    end
    # Process based on the parity of t
    if t % 2 == 1 # odd time: move
      directions.each do |di, dj|
        ni = i + di
        nj = j + dj
        # Check if the new cell is within bounds
        if ni >= 0 && ni < m && nj >= 0 && nj < n
          new_time = t + 1
          new_cost = cost + (ni+1) * (nj+1)
          key_new = [ni, nj, new_time]
          if visited[key_new].nil? || visited[key_new] > new_cost
            visited[key_new] = new_cost
            pq << [new_cost, ni, nj, new_time]
          end
        end
      end
    else # even time: wait
      new_time = t + 1
      new_cost = cost + waitCost[i][j]
      key_new = [i, j, new_time]
      if visited[key_new].nil? || visited[key_new] > new_cost
        visited[key_new] = new_cost
        pq << [new_cost, i, j, new_time]
      end
    end
  end
  # If the queue is empty and destination not found (should not happen)
  return -1
end

def solve(m, n, waitCost)
  # Initialize the priority queue
  pq = []
  # Initial state: (0,0) at time 1, cost is 1
  # The cost is entry cost of (0,0)
  initial_cost = (0+1) * (0+1)
  # Push (cost, i, j, time)
  pq << [initial_cost, 0, 0, 1]
  # Track the minimal cost for each (i,j, time)
  # Since Ruby doesn't have a 3D array, we can use a hash
  visited = Hash.new
  # The visited hash will store the minimal cost for (i,j, time)
  # So, for each (i,j, time), the value is the minimal cost to reach it
  # We can use a key like [i, j, time]
  # Initialize the visited with the initial state
  visited[[0,0,1]] = initial_cost
  # Directions for movement: right and down
  directions = [[0,1], [1,0]]
  # While the queue is not empty
  while pq.length > 0
    # Sort the queue to get the minimal cost
    pq.sort!
    # Pop the first element
    current = pq.shift
    cost, i, j, t = current
    # Check if we reached the destination
    if i == m-1 && j == n-1
      return cost
    end
    # Check if this state has been processed with a lower cost
    # If the current cost is higher than the stored one, skip
    key = [i, j, t]
    if visited[key] && visited[key] < cost
      next
    end
    # Process based on the parity of t
    if t % 2 == 1 # odd time: move
      # Check possible moves
      directions.each do |di, dj|
        ni = i + di
        nj = j + dj
        # Check if the new cell is within bounds
        if ni >= 0 && ni < m && nj >= 0 && nj < n
          new_time = t + 1
          new_cost = cost + (ni+1) * (nj+1)
          # Check if this state is already in visited with a lower cost
          key_new = [ni, nj, new_time]
          if visited[key_new].nil? || visited[key_new] > new_cost
            visited[key_new] = new_cost
            pq << [new_cost, ni, nj, new_time]
          end
        end
      end
    else # even time: wait
      # Wait in the current cell
      new_time = t + 1
      new_cost = cost + waitCost[i][j]
      key_new = [i, j, new_time]
      if visited[key_new].nil? || visited[key_new] > new_cost
        visited[key_new] = new_cost
        pq << [new_cost, i, j, new_time]
      end
    end
  end
  # If the queue is empty and destination not found (should not happen)
  return -1
end

def solve(m, n, waitCost)
  pq = []
  # Initial state: (0,0) at time 1, cost is (0+1)*(0+1) = 1
  initial_cost = (0+1) * (0+1)
  pq << [initial_cost, 0, 0, 1]
  visited = {}
  visited[[0, 0, 1]] = initial_cost
  directions = [[0, 1], [1, 0]]
  while pq.length > 0
    # Sort the queue to get the minimal cost
    pq.sort!
    current = pq.shift
    cost, i, j, t = current
    # Check if we reached the destination
    if i == m-1 && j == n-1
      return cost
    end
    # Check if this state has been processed with a lower cost
    key = [i, j, t]
    if visited[key] && visited[key] < cost
      next
    end
    # Process based on the parity of t
    if t % 2 == 1 # odd time: move
      directions.each do |di, dj|
        ni = i + di
        nj = j + dj
        # Check if the new cell is within bounds
        if ni >= 0 && ni < m && nj >= 0 && nj < n
          new_time = t + 1
          new_cost = cost + (ni+1) * (nj+1)
          key_new = [ni, nj, new_time]
          if visited[key_new].nil? || visited[key_new] > new_cost
            visited[key_new] = new_cost
            pq << [new_cost, ni, nj, new_time]
          end
        end
      end
    else # even time: wait
      new_time = t + 1
      new_cost = cost + waitCost[i][j]
      key_new = [i, j, new_time]
      if visited[key_new].nil? || visited[key_new] > new_cost
        visited[key_new] = new_cost
        pq << [new_cost, i, j, new_time]
      end
    end
  end
  # If the queue is empty and destination not found (should not happen)
  return -1
end

  # Initialize the priority queue

def solve(m, n, waitCost)
  # Initialize the priority queue
  pq = []
  # Initial state: (0,0) at time 1, cost is 1
  # The cost is entry cost of (0,0)
  initial_cost = (0+1) * (0+1)
  # Push (cost, i, j, time)

  # Track the minimal cost for each (i,j, time)
  # Since Ruby doesn't have a 3D array, we can use a hash
  visited = Hash.new
  # The visited hash will store the minimal cost for (i,j, time)
  # So, for each (i,j, time), the value is the minimal cost to reach it
  # We can use a key like [i, j, time]
  # Initialize the visited with the initial state
  visited[[0,0,1]] = initial_cost
  # Directions for movement: right and down
  directions = [[0,1], [1,0]]
  # While the queue is not empty
  while pq.length > 0
    # Sort the queue to get the minimal cost
    pq.sort!
    # Pop the first element
    current = pq.shift
    cost, i, j, t = current
    # Check if we reached the destination
    if i == m-1 && j == n-1
      return cost
    end
    # Check if this state has been processed with a lower cost
    # If the current cost is higher than the stored one, skip
    key = [i, j, t]
    if visited[key] && visited[key] < cost
      next
    end
    # Process based on the parity of t
    if t % 2 == 1 # odd time: move
      # Check possible moves
      directions.each do |di, dj|
        ni = i + di
        nj = j + dj
        # Check if the new cell is within bounds
        if ni >= 0 && ni < m && nj >= 0 && nj < n
          new_time = t + 1
          new_cost = cost + (ni+1) * (nj+1)
          # Check if this state is already in visited with a lower cost
          key_new = [ni, nj, new_time]
          if visited[key_new].nil? || visited[key_new] > new_cost
            visited[key_new] = new_cost
            pq << [new_cost, ni, nj, new_time]
          end
        end
      end
    else # even time: wait
      # Wait in the current cell
      new_time = t + 1
      new_cost = cost + waitCost[i][j]
      key_new = [i, j, new_time]
      if visited[key_new].nil? || visited[key_new] > new_cost

        pq << [new_cost, i, j, new_time]
      end
    end
  end
  # If the queue is empty and destination not found (should not happen)
  return -1
